title: Pentameris minor
taxonomy: Pentameris minor is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Pentameris, which is a group of grasses native to southern Africa. Pentameris minor is further classified as belonging to the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. The species name 'minor' refers to its relatively small stature. Pentameris minor is a type of cool-season grass, meaning it grows best in cooler temperatures.
uses: Pentameris minor is also known as little wood grass, which is native to Africa and parts of Asia. Pentameris minor has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever and sore throats. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, making it a potential remedy for respiratory problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal applications and potential health benefits.

intro: Pentameris minor, also known as Little Wood Grass or Wood Grass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its delicate foliage and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in relieving digestive issues and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Pentameris minor include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Pentameris minor is often combined with other plants, such as peppermint and chamomile, to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that promote relaxation and soothe digestive issues.
